title: tapinanthus globifer subsp. bangwensis
intro: Tapinanthus globifer subsp. bangwensis, also known as the yellow milkberry, is a parasitic plant species in the Loranthaceae family that grows on the branches of trees in tropical Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tapinanthus globifer subsp. bangwensis include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to create a medicinal tonic, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a treatment for various health conditions.
